sentence: Subjective:

HPI: The patient has been experiencing headaches for years, starting in her teenage years, initially associated with her menstrual periods, but now occurring more frequently and severely, with a pulsing sensation on the right side behind her eye.
The headaches worsen with exercise, particularly jogging.
She also reports a ringing in her ears for a few months and recent onset of blurred vision, particularly on the side of her vision.
The patient denies numbness or tingling in her hands or feet.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of chronic headaches, but no other relevant medical history or social history was mentioned.
Review of systems: The patient reports a ringing in her ears and blurred vision, but denies any other symptoms.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure was within normal limits, but this information was not explicitly stated in the transcript.
Physical exam: The patient's heart exam showed a normal sinus rhythm with no murmurs, rubs, or gallops.
The funduscopic exam revealed papilledema, or swelling behind the eye.
The neurological exam showed intact cranial nerves one through twelve, including a normal cranial reflex.
The musculoskeletal exam showed a normal gait.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has symptoms concerning for idiopathic intracranial hypertension, a condition characterized by increased pressure in the brain without a known cause.
Problem list: Idiopathic intracranial hypertension; confirmed.
Plan:
I prescribed an MRI to check for any other conditions that could be contributing to the patient's symptoms.
I will also be performing a spinal tap to evaluate the pressure in the brain and to potentially alleviate symptoms by removing fluid.
Additionally, I will refer the patient to a neuro-ophthalmologist for a complete eye exam.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with the front office to obtain preapproval for the MRI and then schedule the test.
